name: MIDI 1U
description: 1U USB/DIN MIDI-to-CV interface for Intellijel cases. Converts MIDI pitch, velocity, mod, CC, gate, trigger, and clock to CV. Single or Dual synth mode. Expandable to 10 synths with CVx modules.

controls.name: LEARN Button
controls.description: Cycles through three states to learn MIDI channel and CC/MOD jack assignments.
controls.detail: Off = normal · Solid blue = LEARN CC+CH · Flashing blue = LEARN MOD+CH · long-press = toggle Dual Mode

controls.name: CLK÷ Button
controls.description: Sets MIDI clock division for the CLK output jack. In LEARN mode, toggles CC/MOD jack CV polarity (unipolar/bipolar).

outputs.name: PITCH
outputs.description: 1V/oct CV pitch output for Synth 1. Note 0 (C-2) = -5V, Note 120 (C8) = +5V.

outputs.name: MOD
outputs.description: In Single mode: MIDI CC or aftertouch CV for Synth 1. In Dual mode: Pitch for Synth 2.

outputs.name: GATE
outputs.description: Gate output for Synth 1. High when note is held.

outputs.name: CLK
outputs.description: Divided MIDI clock output. Division set by CLK+ button.

outputs.name: VEL
outputs.description: Velocity CV output for Synth 1, proportional to MIDI velocity.

outputs.name: CC
outputs.description: In Single mode: assignable MIDI CC CV output. In Dual mode: Velocity for Synth 2.

outputs.name: TRIG
outputs.description: In Single mode: note trigger output for Synth 1. In Dual mode: Gate for Synth 2.

outputs.name: RST
outputs.description: RESET output (trigger on MIDI reset) or RUN output (gate high while clock running), configurable in Intellijel Config app.

behaviors.name: Single Mode
behaviors.description: Controls one monophonic synth on a user-selected MIDI channel. Outputs PITCH, VEL, MOD, CC, GATE, TRIG, CLK, RST. CC and MOD jacks assignable to any MIDI CC or aftertouch.

behaviors.name: Dual Mode
behaviors.description: Controls two monophonic synths on separate MIDI channels. PITCH/VEL/GATE = Synth 1; MOD/CC/TRIG repurposed as PITCH/VEL/GATE for Synth 2. LEARN button glows yellow in Dual Mode.

behaviors.name: MIDI Channel Learning
behaviors.description: Module awaits any incoming MIDI message and locks to that channel, also assigning the CC jack to that CC number. Settings auto-saved after 2 seconds of MIDI inactivity.

behaviors.name: Portamento
behaviors.trigger: MIDI CC 65 on / CC 5 for time
behaviors.description: Pitch output slews when portamento is enabled via standard MIDI CC messages. Time set by CC 5.

behaviors.name: CVx Expansion
behaviors.description: Up to 4 CVx expanders add 8 assignable outputs each (32 total extra jacks), enabling polyphonic, drum, and multi-channel operation up to 10 synths on 10 MIDI channels.

patchIdeas.name: Basic mono synth
patchIdeas.patch: PITCH → VCO, GATE → envelope, VEL → VCA for velocity dynamics.
steps: Make sure MIDI is reaching the case (USB or DIN into the MIDI IN Jacks / 7U / Palette).
Press LEARN once — the button glows solid blue.
On your MIDI controller, play a note on the channel you want (e.g. Ch 1). LEARN turns off; Synth 1 is locked to that channel.
Patch MIDI 1U PITCH → VCO 1V/oct input.
Patch MIDI 1U GATE → envelope Trig/Gate in.
Patch the envelope Out → VCA CV in.
Patch MIDI 1U VEL → a second VCA CV in (or the same VCA's second CV) so loud notes get louder.
Play the keyboard — each note should track pitch and open the envelope.
patchIdeas.signalOut: PITCH: ±5V 1V/oct · GATE: 0–5V while note held · VEL: 0–5V proportional to velocity.
patchIdeas.hear: A standard monosynth that tracks your keyboard and gets louder the harder you play.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIDI 1U:
- MIDI 1U (PITCH) p> VCO (1V/oct)
- MIDI 1U (GATE) g> Envelope (Trig)
- MIDI 1U (VEL) >> VCA (CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV) // velocity-sensitive dynamics

patchIdeas.name: Dual synth layering
patchIdeas.patch: Dual Mode repurposes MOD/CC/TRIG as PITCH/VEL/GATE for Synth 2.
steps: Long-press LEARN (>2 sec) — LEARN glows yellow, module is now in Dual Mode.
Press LEARN once (it turns red), then play a note on your Synth 1 channel (e.g. Ch 1). LEARN returns to yellow.
Double-press LEARN (it flashes red), then play a note on your Synth 2 channel (e.g. Ch 2). LEARN returns to yellow.
Patch PITCH → VCO 1 (1V/oct) and GATE → Env 1 Trig for Synth 1.
Patch MOD → VCO 2 (1V/oct) — this is now Synth 2's pitch.
Patch TRIG → Env 2 Trig — this is now Synth 2's gate.
Optionally patch VEL → VCA 1 CV and CC → VCA 2 CV (CC is Synth 2 velocity in Dual Mode).
Play both channels from your DAW/controller — two independent mono voices.
patchIdeas.signalOut: PITCH & MOD: ±5V 1V/oct (Synth 1 & 2 pitch) · GATE & TRIG: 0–5V (Synth 1 & 2 gates).
patchIdeas.hear: Two separate monosynths respond to two MIDI channels — great for bass + lead, or layered unison with detuned VCOs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIDI 1U:
* Mode: Dual (long-press LEARN)
- MIDI 1U (PITCH) p> VCO 1 (1V/oct)
- MIDI 1U (GATE) g> Env 1 (Trig)
- MIDI 1U (MOD) p> VCO 2 (1V/oct) // Synth 2 pitch in Dual Mode
- MIDI 1U (TRIG) g> Env 2 (Trig) // Synth 2 gate in Dual Mode

patchIdeas.name: MIDI clock to sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: CLK at /24 = quarter notes; RST resets the sequencer on MIDI Start/Reset.
steps: Start MIDI clock from your DAW or hardware sequencer into the case's MIDI IN.
Press CLK+ repeatedly until the LED blinks at quarter-note speed — this is the /24 setting.
Patch MIDI 1U CLK → your sequencer's Clock In.
Patch MIDI 1U RST → your sequencer's Reset input (RST outputs a trigger on MIDI Start/Reset by default).
Press Play in your DAW — the sequencer should snap to beat 1 and advance one step per quarter note.
If you want half notes or 16ths, press CLK+ again to cycle (/48 = half, /6 = 16th, /12 = 8th, etc.).
patchIdeas.signalOut: CLK: 0–5V gate pulses at the selected division · RST: 0–5V trigger on MIDI Start/Reset.
patchIdeas.hear: External sequencers lock to your DAW's tempo and restart cleanly on every play.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIDI 1U:
* CLK+: /24 (quarter notes)
- MIDI 1U (CLK) c> Sequencer (Clock In)
- MIDI 1U (RST) t> Sequencer (Reset)

patchIdeas.name: Mod wheel filter sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Assign MOD jack to mod wheel (CC 1) and send it to filter cutoff.
steps: Set up a basic mono patch first: PITCH → VCO, GATE → envelope → VCA.
Press LEARN twice — the button flashes blue (LEARN MOD+CH mode).
On your controller, move the mod wheel on the channel you want (e.g. Ch 1). LEARN turns off; MOD jack is now assigned to CC 1.
Patch MIDI 1U MOD → filter cutoff CV input.
Set the filter cutoff manually to a fairly closed position so there's room to open up.
Play and push the mod wheel up — cutoff opens. Pull it down — cutoff closes.
patchIdeas.signalOut: MOD: 0–5V unipolar (default) proportional to mod wheel position.
patchIdeas.hear: Hands-on filter sweep from the mod wheel while you play — classic expressive mono-synth performance.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIDI 1U:
* LEARN: MOD+CH (flashing blue, move mod wheel)
- MIDI 1U (PITCH) p> VCO (1V/oct)
- MIDI 1U (GATE) g> Envelope (Trig)
- MIDI 1U (MOD) >> Filter (Cutoff) // mod wheel sweeps filter
